entries.summary: Phase 6 Test Suite Audit complete. Audited unit tests (6 files, 16 findings), regression tests (6 files, 10 findings), orphan files (4 files, 4 findings). Total 30 findings (1 CRITICAL, 8 HIGH, 14 MEDIUM, 7 LOW). CRITICAL: inverted assertion in regression test passes when fix is reverted. Major pattern: regression tests use string inspection instead of runtime verification. Architect caught 1 false positive (unittest-001 assertion placement).

metadata.plan_id: Comprehensive Codebase QA Review — AI-Hardened Edition

lessons_learned: Regression test quality is fundamentally different from unit test quality — the key question is 'does this test fail when the fix is reverted?' not 'does this test pass?'
Inverted assertions are the most dangerous regression test anti-pattern — a test that passes when the fix is broken provides false confidence
String inspection tests (inspect.getsource) are a common anti-pattern in regression suites — they verify source text exists but not that it's wired correctly at runtime
Over-mocking in regression tests defeats their purpose — if you mock away the fix, the test cannot detect re-introduction of the defect
Orphan files are easy to detect (diff) but indicate process gaps — tests were likely created at root during development and never moved to tests/
